Q28 = made a suicide plan. Q29 = attempted suicide. Q19 = forced sexual intercourse. Q57 = age of first sexual intercourse (dichotomized as age 12 or younger). Q91 = parental intimate partner violence. Q100 = household substance use. Q101 = household mental illness. Q14 = unsafe school absences. Q15 = weapon threats at school. Q18 = unsafe neighborhood.")
```{r table-s1-reliability, message=FALSE, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
ft_s5
save_as_docx(ft_s5, path = here::here("figures", "TableS9_Reliability.docx"))
```

```{r table-s2-subsample, message=FALSE, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# CHUNK 36: TABLE S10 — SUBSAMPLE VALIDATION
# Purpose: Compare M3 estimates between the full sample and national-module subsample to verify that the two analytic samples yield comparable results.
# Notes: AOR change < 4.3% for all predictors confirms subsample comparability. This is Table S10.
# ============================================================

# TABLE S10: Subsample Validation
# Compares M3 results on full sample vs national-module subsample.
###############################################################################

val_terms <- c("q2_1Female",
               "q3_110th", "q3_111th", "q3_112th",
               "q4_1Black", "q4_1Latine/Multiple Latine",
               "q4_1Multiple Non-Latine", "q4_1Other",
               "su_lifeYes", "rb_1Yes", "bu_1Yes", "sc_1Yes")
val_labels <- c("Female sex",
                "Grade 10th", "Grade 11th", "Grade 12th",
                "Black", "Latine/Multiple Latine",
                "Multiple Non-Latine", "Other race/ethnicity",
                "Lifetime polysubstance use", "Risk behavior",
                "Bullying victimization", "Suicidality")

s2_df <- data.frame(
  Predictor = val_labels,
  Full_AOR = sapply(val_terms, function(t) sprintf("%.2f", res_M3$AOR[res_M3$term == t])),
  Sub_AOR = sapply(val_terms, function(t) sprintf("%.2f", res_M3_sub$AOR[res_M3_sub$term == t])),
  Change = sapply(val_terms, function(t) {
    a <- res_M3$AOR[res_M3$term == t]
    b <- res_M3_sub$AOR[res_M3_sub$term == t]
    sprintf("%.1f", abs(a - b) / a * 100)
  }),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, row.names = NULL
)
names(s2_df) <- c("Predictor", "Full Sample AOR", "Subsample AOR", "Change (%)")

s3_notes <- paste0(
  "Full sample N = ", format(attr(res_M3, "n"), big.mark = ","),
  ". National-module subsample N = ", format(attr(res_M3_sub, "n"), big.mark = ","),
  ". Change calculated as absolute percentage change in AOR. ",
  "All changes fall below the pre-specified 15% flag threshold, confirming analytic sample comparability. ",
  "Maximum AOR change = ", sprintf("%.1f", max_aor_change), "%."
)
ft_s6 <- ft_out(s2_df,
  cap = "Table S10. Subsample Validation: M3 Estimated on Full Sample Versus National-Module Subsample",
  align_spec = c("l", "c", "c", "c"),
  notes = s3_notes)
ft_s6
save_as_docx(ft_s6, path = here::here("figures", "TableS10_SubsampleValidation.docx"))
```

```{r table-s3-khb, message=FALSE, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# CHUNK 37: TABLE S11 — KHB MEDIATION DECOMPOSITION
# Purpose: Display the proportion of each predictor's NMPOU association mediated by lifetime polysubstance involvement.
# Notes: KHB corrects for noncollapsibility. Bullying: 10.3% mediated; sv_1: 25.2% mediated (highest). Reference: Karlson, Holm & Breen (2012). This is Table S11.
# ============================================================

# TABLE S11: KHB Mediation Decomposition
# Uses proper labels (not variable names like bu_1) per comment 28.
###############################################################################

s3_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = c("Bullying victimization", "Suicidality", "Sexual victimization",
               "Risk behavior", "Household adversity", "Community safety adversity"),
  Total_AOR = round(exp(c(beta_total_bu, beta_total_sc, beta_total_sv,
                           beta_total_rb, beta_total_hh, beta_total_ms)), 2),
  Direct_AOR = round(exp(c(beta_direct_bu, beta_direct_sc, beta_direct_sv,
                            beta_direct_rb, beta_direct_hh, beta_direct_ms)), 2),
  Indirect = round(c(indirect_bu, indirect_sc, indirect_sv,
                      indirect_rb, indirect_hh, indirect_ms), 3),
  Pct_Mediated = round(c(prop_med_bu, prop_med_sc, prop_med_sv,
                          prop_med_rb, prop_med_hh, prop_med_ms), 1),
  Pct_Med_CI = paste0(
    sprintf("%.1f", khb_ci_df$CI_lo), " to ",
    sprintf("%.1f", khb_ci_df$CI_hi)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE
)
names(s3_df) <- c("Variable", "Total AOR", "Direct AOR",
                   "Indirect (log-odds)", "% Mediated", "95% CI")

s4_khb_notes <- paste0(
  "KHB = Karlson-Holm-Breen decomposition. Total effect = association before adjusting for polysubstance ",
  "involvement (from model with demographics + all other predictors, replacing su_life with its residuals). ",
  "Direct effect = association after adjusting for polysubstance involvement (from model with su_life included). ",
  "Indirect effect = total minus direct on the log-odds scale. Proportion mediated = indirect / total on the log-odds scale. ",
  "Cross-sectional KHB results are interpreted as accounting decompositions, not causal mediation pathways. ",
  "Bullying, suicidality, sexual victimization, and risk behavior: N = ", format(n_khb, big.mark = ","),
  " complete cases (full sample). ",
  "(a) Household adversity and community safety adversity: N = ", format(n_khb_sub, big.mark = ","),
  " complete cases (national-module subsample)."
)
# Add superscript "a" to Household and Community rows
khb_ft <- ft_out(s3_df,
  cap = "Table S11. KHB Mediation Decomposition: Proportion of Predictor-NMPOU Associations Mediated by Lifetime Polysubstance Involvement",
  align_spec = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c"),
  notes = s4_khb_notes)
khb_ft <- compose(khb_ft, i = 5, j = 1, value = as_paragraph("Household adversity", as_sup("a")))
khb_ft <- compose(khb_ft, i = 6, j = 1, value = as_paragraph("Community safety adversity", as_sup("a")))
khb_ft
save_as_docx(khb_ft, path = here::here("figures", "TableS11_KHB.docx"))
```

```{r table-s4-stacked, message=FALSE, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# CHUNK 38: TABLE S12 — STACKED CONFIRMATORY MODEL
# Purpose: Display key interaction coefficients from the three-substance confirmatory stacked model (NMPOU, alcohol, marijuana).
# Notes: Confirmatory model restricts to three substances with adequate prevalence. Tests whether predictor-substance interactions are significant. This is Table S12.
# ============================================================

# TABLE S12: Stacked-Outcome Heterogeneity Test Interaction Coefficients
# Extracted from the 3-substance confirmatory stacked model.
###############################################################################

# Extract interaction coefficients from 3-substance model (all variables)
int_terms_3 <- c("outcome_typeAlcohol:bu_1Yes", "outcome_typeMarijuana:bu_1Yes",
                 "outcome_typeAlcohol:sc_1Yes", "outcome_typeMarijuana:sc_1Yes",
                 "outcome_typeAlcohol:sv_1Yes", "outcome_typeMarijuana:sv_1Yes",
                 "outcome_typeAlcohol:rb_1Yes", "outcome_typeMarijuana:rb_1Yes",
                 "outcome_typeAlcohol:hh_1Yes", "outcome_typeMarijuana:hh_1Yes",
                 "outcome_typeAlcohol:ms_1Yes", "outcome_typeMarijuana:ms_1Yes")

int_labels_3 <- c("Bullying x Alcohol", "Bullying x Marijuana",
                   "Suicidality x Alcohol", "Suicidality x Marijuana",
                   "Sexual victimization x Alcohol", "Sexual victimization x Marijuana",
                   "Risk behavior x Alcohol", "Risk behavior x Marijuana",
                   "Household adversity x Alcohol", "Household adversity x Marijuana",
                   "Community safety x Alcohol", "Community safety x Marijuana")

# Only include terms that exist in the model output
valid_idx <- int_terms_3 %in% rownames(cc3)
s4_df <- data.frame(
  Interaction = int_labels_3[valid_idx],
  Beta = sprintf("%.2f", cc3[int_terms_3[valid_idx], "Estimate"]),
  SE = sprintf("%.2f", cc3[int_terms_3[valid_idx], "Std. Error"]),
  p = sapply(cc3[int_terms_3[valid_idx], "Pr(>|t|)"], function(p) format.pval(p, digits = 3)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, row.names = NULL
)

s5_stacked_notes <- paste0(
  "NMPOU is the reference outcome. Negative interaction coefficients indicate that the variable's ",
  "association is stronger for NMPOU than for the comparison substance. Positive coefficients indicate the reverse. ",
  "Joint Wald test of F2 distress interactions (bullying + suicidality, 4 terms): F = ", round(wald_all3$Ftest, 2),
  ", p = ", format.pval(wald_all3$p, digits = 4),
  ". Sexual victimization interactions are included in the table for completeness but tested separately as an F3 variable."
)
ft_s8 <- ft_out(s4_df,
  cap = "Table S12. Three-Substance Confirmatory Model: Key Interaction Coefficients",
  align_spec = c("l", "c", "c", "c"),
  notes = s5_stacked_notes)
ft_s8
save_as_docx(ft_s8, path = here::here("figures", "TableS12_Stacked.docx"))
```

```{r table-s5-attenuation, message=FALSE, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# CHUNK 39: TABLE S13 — ATTENUATION ACROSS MODEL SEQUENCE
# Purpose: Track how predictor associations change from M2a/M3 through M5 to quantify attenuation due to added covariates.
# Notes: Attenuation quantifies how much of each association operates through shared ecological context. Bullying: 36.5% attenuation; suicidality: 21.1%. This is Table S13.
# ============================================================

# TABLE S13: Attenuation of Distress Associations Across Model Sequence
###############################################################################

# Helper: safely extract AOR (returns NA if term not in model)
safe_aor <- function(res, term) {
  row <- res[res$term == term, ]
  if (nrow(row) == 0) return(NA)
  row$AOR
}

att_vars <- c("su_lifeYes", "rb_1Yes", "bu_1Yes", "sc_1Yes", "sv_1Yes", "hh_1Yes", "ms_1Yes")
att_labels <- c("Lifetime polysubstance use", "Risk behavior",
                "Bullying victimization", "Suicidality", "Sexual victimization",
                "Household adversity", "Community safety adversity")

s5_df <- data.frame(
  Variable = att_labels,
  M2a = sapply(att_vars, function(t) {
    v <- safe_aor(res_M2a, t); ifelse(is.na(v), "--", sprintf("%.2f", v)) }),
  M3 = sapply(att_vars, function(t) {
    v <- safe_aor(res_M3, t); ifelse(is.na(v), "--", sprintf("%.2f", v)) }),
  M3_sub = sapply(att_vars, function(t) {
    v <- safe_aor(res_M3_sub, t); ifelse(is.na(v), "--", sprintf("%.2f", v)) }),
  M4 = sapply(att_vars, function(t) {
    v <- safe_aor(res_M4, t); ifelse(is.na(v), "--", sprintf("%.2f", v)) }),
  M5 = sapply(att_vars, function(t) {
    v <- safe_aor(res_M5, t); ifelse(is.na(v), "--", sprintf("%.2f", v)) }),
  Att_M2a_M3 = sapply(att_vars, function(t) {
    v <- compute_attenuation(res_M2a, res_M3, t); ifelse(is.na(v), "--", paste0(v, "%")) }),
  Att_M3sub_M5 = sapply(att_vars, function(t) {
    v <- compute_attenuation(res_M3_sub, res_M5, t); ifelse(is.na(v), "--", paste0(v, "%")) }),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, row.names = NULL
)
names(s5_df) <- c("Variable", "M2a AOR", "M3 AOR", "M3-sub AOR",
                   "M4 AOR", "M5 AOR", "M2a to M3 (%)", "M3-sub to M5 (%)")

ft_s9 <- ft_out(s5_df,
  cap = "Table S13. Attenuation of Predictor Associations Across Model Sequence",
  align_spec = c("l", rep("c", 7)),
  notes = "Attenuation calculated on the log-odds scale as (beta_earlier - beta_later) / beta_earlier x 100, where beta is the raw regression coefficient. This avoids distortion from rounding when using exponentiated AORs. M2a = demographics + distress (full sample). M3 = M2a + polysubstance use + risk behavior (full sample). M3-sub = M3 estimated on national-module subsample. M4 = M3-sub + household adversity. M5 = M4 + community safety adversity. All models adjust for sex, grade, and race/ethnicity.")
ft_s9
save_as_docx(ft_s9, path = here::here("figures", "TableS13_Attenuation.docx"))
```

```{r table-nmpou-comparison, message=FALSE, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# CHUNK 40: TABLE S14 — NMPOU+ vs NMPOU- COMPARISON
# Purpose: Compare survey-weighted prevalence of demographic and predictor characteristics between NMPOU-positive and NMPOU-negative adolescents.
# Notes: Uses svychisq() for significance testing. Provides the descriptive foundation for understanding who endorses NMPOU. This is Table S14.
# ============================================================

# TABLE S14: NMPOU+ vs NMPOU- Sample Characteristics
# Purpose: Compare survey-weighted prevalence of demographics and composite
#          predictors between NMPOU-positive and NMPOU-negative adolescents.
#          Uses svychisq() for design-adjusted chi-square tests.
###############################################################################

# Split survey designs by NMPOU status
des_pos <- subset(des_full, q49_1 == "Yes")
des_neg <- subset(des_full, q49_1 == "No")
des_pos_sub <- subset(des_sub, q49_1 == "Yes")
des_neg_sub <- subset(des_sub, q49_1 == "No")

# Helper: compute weighted percentage of "Yes" in a binary variable
wt_yes_pct <- function(des, var_name) {
  frml <- as.formula(paste0("~", var_name))
  tryCatch({
    val <- 100 * coef(svymean(frml, des, na.rm = TRUE))[paste0(var_name, "Yes")]
    sprintf("%.1f", val)
  }, error = function(e) "--")
}

# Helper: compute weighted percentage of a specific level
wt_level_pct <- function(des, var_name, level) {
  frml <- as.formula(paste0("~", var_name))
  tryCatch({
    val <- 100 * coef(svymean(frml, des, na.rm = TRUE))[paste0(var_name, level)]
    sprintf("%.1f", val)
  }, error = function(e) "--")
}

# Helper: design-adjusted chi-square p-value
chi_p <- function(des, var_name) {
  frml <- as.formula(paste0("~", var_name, " + q49_1"))
  tryCatch({
    test <- svychisq(frml, des, statistic = "Chisq")
    p <- test$p.value
    if (p < 0.001) return("< .001")
    sprintf("%.3f", p)
  }, error = function(e) "--")
}

# Build comparison rows
comp_rows <- list()

# Demographics
comp_rows[[1]] <- data.frame(
  Variable = "Female sex", NMPOU_pos = wt_level_pct(des_pos, "q2_1", "Female"),
  NMPOU_neg = wt_level_pct(des_neg, "q2_1", "Female"),
  p = chi_p(des_full, "q2_1"), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Race/ethnicity
for (lev in c("White", "Black", "Latine/Multiple Latine",
              "Multiple Non-Latine", "Other")) {
  comp_rows[[length(comp_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    Variable = paste0("  ", lev),
    NMPOU_pos = wt_level_pct(des_pos, "q4_1", lev),
    NMPOU_neg = wt_level_pct(des_neg, "q4_1", lev),
    p = if (lev == "White") chi_p(des_full, "q4_1") else "",
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

# Composite predictors (full sample)
for (v in c("su_life", "rb_1", "bu_1", "sc_1", "sv_1")) {
  lab <- c(su_life = "Polysubstance use", rb_1 = "Risk behavior",
           bu_1 = "Bullying victimization", sc_1 = "Suicidality",
           sv_1 = "Sexual victimization")[v]
  comp_rows[[length(comp_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    Variable = lab, NMPOU_pos = wt_yes_pct(des_pos, v),
    NMPOU_neg = wt_yes_pct(des_neg, v),
    p = chi_p(des_full, v), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

# Composite predictors (subsample)
for (v in c("hh_1", "ms_1")) {
  lab <- c(hh_1 = "Household adversity", ms_1 = "Community safety adversity")[v]
  comp_rows[[length(comp_rows) + 1]] <- data.frame(
    Variable = lab, NMPOU_pos = wt_yes_pct(des_pos_sub, v),
    NMPOU_neg = wt_yes_pct(des_neg_sub, v),
    p = chi_p(des_sub, v), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
}

comp_df <- bind_rows(comp_rows)
names(comp_df) <- c("Variable", "NMPOU+ (%)", "NMPOU- (%)", "p-value")

# Insert race/ethnicity header
race_header <- data.frame(
  "Variable" = "Race/Ethnicity", "NMPOU+ (%)" = "", "NMPOU- (%)" = "",
  "p-value" = chi_p(des_full, "q4_1"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE, check.names = FALSE)

# Rebuild with header row
comp_final <- rbind(
  comp_df[1, ],                # Female sex
  race_header,                  # Race/Ethnicity header
  comp_df[2:6, ],              # Race/ethnicity levels
  comp_df[7:nrow(comp_df), ]   # Composites
)

s13_notes <- paste0(
  "Percentages are survey-weighted. p-values from design-adjusted Rao-Scott ",
  "chi-square tests. Full-sample predictors: polysubstance use, risk behavior, ",
  "bullying, suicidality, sexual victimization. National-module subsample ",
  "predictors: household adversity, community safety adversity."
)

ft_s13 <- ft_out(comp_final,
  cap = "Table S14. Survey-Weighted Characteristics of NMPOU-Positive vs. NMPOU-Negative Adolescents",
  align_spec = c("l", "c", "c", "c"),
  notes = s13_notes)
ft_s13
save_as_docx(ft_s13, path = here::here("figures", "TableS14_NMPOU_Comparison.docx"))
```

```{r figure3-by-sex, message=FALSE, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=7, fig.height=9}
# ============================================================
# CHUNK 41: FIGURE S1 — FRAMEWORK ENDORSEMENT BY SEX
# Purpose: Create sex-stratified three-framework Euler diagrams for NMPOU-positive adolescents.
# Notes: Female adolescents show consistently larger F1-F2-F3 intersections than males, consistent with differential internalizing/externalizing pathways (Heitzeg et al., 2018). Saved as 300 DPI TIFF.
# ============================================================

# FIGURE S1: Three-Framework Euler Diagrams by Race/Ethnicity, Stratified by Sex
# Layout: 2 rows (Female, Male) x 5 equal-width columns + legend
#
# Depends on: make_euler_3fw(), safe_pct(), col_f1/f2/f3,
#             eth_groups, eth_labels, nmpou_pos_sub, pL_c
###############################################################################

sex_levels <- c("Female", "Male")
n_female <- sum(nmpou_pos_sub$q2_1 == "Female", na.rm = TRUE)
n_male   <- sum(nmpou_pos_sub$q2_1 == "Male",   na.rm = TRUE)

fig_s1_grobs <- list()

for (s in seq_along(sex_levels)) {
  sex_label <- sex_levels[s]
  sex_data <- nmpou_pos_sub[!is.na(nmpou_pos_sub$q2_1) &
                              nmpou_pos_sub$q2_1 == sex_label, ]
  sex_data <- sex_data[complete.cases(sex_data[, c("psu", "stratum", "weight")]), ]
  sex_des  <- svydesign(id = ~psu, strata = ~stratum, weights = ~weight,
                         data = sex_data, nest = TRUE)

  sex_grobs <- lapply(seq_along(eth_groups), function(i) {
    ed  <- make_euler_3fw(sex_des, eth_groups[i])
    fit <- euler(ed$euler_input, shape = "circle")
    qty <- paste0(ed$euler_input, "%")

    plot(fit,
      fills      = list(fill = c(col_f1, col_f2, col_f3), alpha = 0.4),
      edges      = list(col  = c(col_f1, col_f2, col_f3), lwd = 2),
      labels     = list(fontsize = 8, font = 2),
      quantities = list(fontsize = 7),
      main       = list(
        label    = paste0(eth_labels[i], "\n(n = ",
                          format(ed$n, big.mark = ","), ")"),
        fontsize = 8, font = 2))
  })
  fig_s1_grobs[[s]] <- sex_grobs
}

pS1_F <- lapply(fig_s1_grobs[[1]], function(g) {
  wrap_elements(g) + theme(plot.margin = margin(1, 3, 1, 3))
})
pS1_M <- lapply(fig_s1_grobs[[2]], function(g) {
  wrap_elements(g) + theme(plot.margin = margin(1, 3, 1, 3))
})

col_widths_s1 <- c(1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 0.85)

row_female <- (pS1_F[[1]] | pS1_F[[2]] | pS1_F[[3]] | pS1_F[[4]] | pS1_F[[5]] |
                 pL_c) +
  plot_layout(widths = col_widths_s1)

row_male <- (pS1_M[[1]] | pS1_M[[2]] | pS1_M[[3]] | pS1_M[[4]] | pS1_M[[5]] |
               pL_c) +
  plot_layout(widths = col_widths_s1)

fig_s1_plot <- (row_female / row_male) +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1)) +
  plot_annotation(
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Top: Female (n = ", format(n_female, big.mark = ","), ")        ",
      "Bottom: Male (n = ", format(n_male, big.mark = ","), ")"),
    caption = paste(strwrap(paste0(
      "Three-framework Euler diagrams by race/ethnicity, stratified by sex. ",
      "F1 = polysubstance use + risk behavior; ",
      "F2 = bullying + suicidality; ",
      "F3 = sexual victimization + household adversity + community safety. ",
      "National-module subsample only."), width = 80), collapse = "\n"),
    theme = theme(
      plot.subtitle = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9, family = "sans",
                                    margin = margin(t = 3, b = 2)),
      plot.caption  = element_text(size = 7.5, hjust = 0, family = "sans",
                                    lineheight = 1.1, margin = margin(t = 2)),
      plot.margin   = margin(t = 3, r = 3, b = 3, l = 3)))

print(fig_s1_plot)

invisible({
  tiff(here::here("figures", "FigureS1_FrameworkEndorsement_BySex.tiff"),
       width = 7, height = 9, units = "in", res = 300, compression = "lzw")
  print(fig_s1_plot)
  dev.off()
})
```

```{r table-s6-sensitivity, message=FALSE, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# CHUNK 42: TABLE S15 — SENSITIVITY ANALYSES SUMMARY (SM1-SM11)
# Purpose: Display key results from all eleven sensitivity analyses in a single summary table.
# Notes: SM1-SM11 test robustness across alternative operationalizations, subsamples, and outcome definitions. This table MUST be the last element in the supplement. This is Table S15.
# ============================================================

# TABLE S15: Sensitivity Analyses Summary (SM1-SM11)
# Redesigned with "Variable Tested" column and comprehensive notes
###############################################################################

# Helper to safely extract AOR and CI for a given term
fmt_sens <- function(res, term) {
  row <- res[res$term == term, ]
  if (nrow(row) == 0) return(list(aor = "--", ci = "--"))
  list(aor = sprintf("%.2f", row$AOR),
       ci = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", row$CI_lo), "-", sprintf("%.2f", row$CI_hi)))
}

# MODIFIED IN v3 — Table S15 restructured to match ground truth SM1-SM10 definitions.
# MODIFIED IN v4 — SM10 implemented (cocaine polysubstance); SM11 added (separate weapon/fighting).
sm_rows <- list(
  list(m = "SM1", mod = "Persistent sadness replaces suicidality", vt = "Persistent sadness (Q26)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_S1, "q26_1Yes"), n = format(attr(res_S1, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  list(m = "SM2", mod = "Dating violence added (daters only)", vt = "Dating violence (Q21, Q22)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_S2, "dv_1Yes"), n = format(attr(res_S2, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  list(m = "SM3", mod = "Forced sexual intercourse added to M5", vt = "Forced sexual intercourse (Q19)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_S3, "q19_1Yes"), n = format(attr(res_S3, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  list(m = "SM4", mod = "Expanded household adversity (+ Q102)", vt = "Expanded household adversity (Q91, Q100, Q101, Q102)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_S4, "hh_2Yes"), n = format(attr(res_S4, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  list(m = "SM5a", mod = "Early debut added (age 12 or younger)", vt = "Early debut (12 or younger)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_S5, "q57_512 or less"), n = format(attr(res_S5, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  list(m = "SM5b", mod = "Early debut added (age 13 or older)", vt = "Early debut (13 or older)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_S5, "q57_513 or more"), n = ""),
  list(m = "SM6", mod = "Past-30-day polysubstance (M3 spec)", vt = "Past-30-day polysubstance use (Q33, Q42, Q48)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_S6, "su_1Yes"), n = format(attr(res_S6, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  list(m = "SM7", mod = "Three-item risk behavior (M3 spec)", vt = "Risk behavior, 3-item (Q10, Q12, Q17)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_S7, "rb_1_3itemYes"), n = format(attr(res_S7, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  # MODIFIED IN v3 — SM8 now matches ground truth: restricted to national-module respondents
  list(m = "SM8", mod = "Restricted to national-module respondents", vt = "Bullying victimization (Q24, Q25)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_M3_sub, "bu_1Yes"), n = format(attr(res_M3_sub, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  # MODIFIED IN v3 — SM9 now matches ground truth: past-30-day NMPOU as outcome (was Q92-M5)
  list(m = "SM9", mod = "Past-30-day NMPOU as outcome (Q92)", vt = "Suicidality (Q27, Q28, Q29)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_Q92_M5, "sc_1Yes"), n = format(attr(res_Q92_M5, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  # MODIFIED IN v4 — SM10 implemented: 4-substance polysubstance composite including cocaine
  list(m = "SM10", mod = "Alternative polysubstance composite including cocaine", vt = "Bullying victimization (Q24, Q25)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_SM10, "bu_1Yes"), n = format(attr(res_SM10, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  # ADDED IN v4 — SM11 separate weapon/fighting predictors
  list(m = "SM11a", mod = "Weapon carrying and physical fighting as separate predictors", vt = "Weapon carrying (Q12)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_SM11, "q12_1Yes"), n = format(attr(res_SM11, "n"), big.mark = ",")),
  list(m = "SM11b", mod = "Weapon carrying and physical fighting as separate predictors", vt = "Physical fighting (Q17)",
       r = fmt_sens(res_SM11, "q17_1Yes"), n = "")
)

s6_df <- data.frame(
  Model = sapply(sm_rows, function(x) x$m),
  Modification = sapply(sm_rows, function(x) x$mod),
  Variable_Tested = sapply(sm_rows, function(x) x$vt),
  AOR = sapply(sm_rows, function(x) x$r$aor),
  CI = sapply(sm_rows, function(x) x$r$ci),
  N = sapply(sm_rows, function(x) x$n),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
names(s6_df) <- c("Model", "Modification", "Variable Tested", "AOR", "95% CI", "N")

# MODIFIED IN v4 — notes updated to include SM10 and SM11 descriptions
s7_sens_notes <- paste0(
  "All models adjust for demographics (sex, grade, race/ethnicity). SM1 through SM5 use lifetime NMPOU (Q49) ",
  "as the outcome and follow the M5 specification unless otherwise noted. SM6 and SM7 modify the M3 specification ",
  "(full sample, no F3 variables). SM8 restricted the sample to national-module respondents (M3 specification). ",
  "SM9 used past-30-day NMPOU (Q92) as the outcome. SM10 tested an alternative polysubstance composite including ",
  "cocaine (cigarettes, alcohol, marijuana, and cocaine). SM11 entered weapon carrying (Q12) and physical fighting ",
  "(Q17) as separate predictors in place of the 2-item risk behavior composite (rb_1). ",
  "SM3 suicidality attenuation after adding forced sexual intercourse: ", att_s3_sc1, "%. ",
  "The 2-item risk behavior composite (SM7) produced a significant association, whereas the 3-item version ",
  "(adding Q10: riding with drunk driver) did not, reflecting measurement sensitivity to composite specification. ",
  "AOR = adjusted odds ratio; CI = confidence interval."
)
ft_s10 <- ft_out(s6_df,
  cap = "Table S15. Sensitivity Analyses Summary (SM1-SM11)",
  align_spec = c("l", "l", "l", "c", "c", "r"),
  notes = s7_sens_notes)
ft_s10
save_as_docx(ft_s10, path = here::here("figures", "TableS15_Sensitivity.docx"))
```

```{r export-csvs, message=FALSE, include=FALSE, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# CHUNK 43: EXPORT CSVs FOR MANUSCRIPT AND SUPPLEMENT RMDs
# Purpose: Write CSV files that Manuscript_v_final_v5.Rmd and
#          SupplementaryMaterial_v_final_v5.Rmd read when knitting.
# ADDED IN v4
# ============================================================

# Helper: append footnote as last row (all columns filled with same text)
write_with_notes <- function(df, path, notes = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(notes)) {
    foot <- setNames(
      data.frame(matrix(notes, nrow = 1, ncol = ncol(df)), stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
      names(df))
    df <- rbind(df, foot)
  }
  write.csv(df, path, row.names = FALSE)
}

# Create output directory inside the repo (CSV outputs land in output/)
csv_dir <- here::here("output", "supplement_tables")
dir.create(csv_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Table 1 (data underlying the main-text Table 1)
write.csv(t1_df, here::here("output", "table1_data.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Tables S1-S3 (model diagnostics)
s1_notes_txt <- "All models estimated using survey-weighted logistic regression (svyglm, quasibinomial family). Full = full YRBS sample; Subsample = national-module subsample with Framework 3 items. All models converged without warnings."
write_with_notes(model_ns, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS1.csv"), s1_notes_txt)

s2_notes_txt <- "VIF computed from the model matrix of the fully adjusted M5 specification. VIF < 5 indicates acceptable multicollinearity; VIF < 10 is the conventional threshold for concern. All seven composite predictors show VIF well below conventional thresholds, indicating that multicollinearity does not threaten coefficient estimation."
write_with_notes(vif_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS2.csv"), s2_notes_txt)

s3_notes_txt <- "Each test evaluates whether the added block of predictors significantly improves model fit beyond the previous specification. All blocks contributed significantly."
write_with_notes(wald_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS3.csv"), s3_notes_txt)

# Table S4 (composite summary)
s4_notes_txt <- "All composites coded dichotomously (0 = no items endorsed; 1 = any item endorsed). Boot CI = percentile bootstrap confidence interval (500 resamples)."
write_with_notes(comp_summary, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS4.csv"), s4_notes_txt)

# Table S5 (primary models)
s5_notes_txt <- "AOR columns show adjusted odds ratios (95% confidence intervals). M5 AME column shows average marginal effects in percentage points (95% CI), representing the average change in predicted P(NMPOU) when the predictor switches from absent to present. M1 = demographics only. M2a = demographics + distress variables (bullying, suicidality). M3 = M2a + lifetime polysubstance use + risk behavior. M4 = M3 + sexual victimization + household adversity (national-module subsample). M5 = M4 + community safety adversity (national-module subsample). Sexual victimization is classified as F3 (contextual adversity) based on formal specificity testing (see Supplementary Material). Reference categories: male sex, 9th grade, White race/ethnicity. \"--\" indicates that the predictor was not included in that model. *p < .05; **p < .01; ***p < .001."
write_with_notes(t2_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS5.csv"), s5_notes_txt)

# Table S6 (drug-specific)
s6_notes_txt <- "Each column reports adjusted odds ratios (95% CI) from the M5 specification applied to a different lifetime substance outcome. LOO = leave-one-out polysubstance composite: the standard three-substance index (cigarettes, alcohol, marijuana) is modified to exclude whichever substance serves as that column's outcome, with NMPOU substituted in its place. For NMPOU and cocaine columns, the standard polysubstance composite is used because neither substance is part of the original index. All models adjust for sex, grade, and race/ethnicity. For probability-scale comparisons using average marginal effects, see Table S7. *p < .05; **p < .01; ***p < .001."
write_with_notes(t3_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS6.csv"), s6_notes_txt)

# Table S7 (AME by substance)
write_with_notes(s11_display, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS7.csv"), s11_notes)

# Table S8 (sv1 framework)
write_with_notes(s12_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS8.csv"), s12_notes)

# Table S9 (reliability)
s9_notes_txt <- "Alpha = tetrachoric alpha. Omega = McDonald's omega estimated from confirmatory factor analysis with weighted least squares mean and variance adjusted (WLSMV) estimation. Spearman-Brown prophecy formula used for two-item composites. 95% Boot CI = percentile bootstrap confidence interval (500 resamples) for the primary metric (alpha for 3+ item composites; Spearman-Brown for 2-item composites). \"--\" indicates not applicable or not estimable."
write_with_notes(s1_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS9.csv"), s9_notes_txt)

# Table S10 (subsample validation)
write_with_notes(s2_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS10.csv"), s3_notes)

# Table S11 (KHB)
write_with_notes(s3_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS11.csv"), s4_khb_notes)

# Table S12 (stacked)
write_with_notes(s4_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS12.csv"), s5_stacked_notes)

# Table S13 (attenuation)
s13_att_notes_txt <- "Attenuation calculated on the log-odds scale as (beta_earlier - beta_later) / beta_earlier x 100, where beta is the raw regression coefficient. This avoids distortion from rounding when using exponentiated AORs. M2a = demographics + distress (full sample). M3 = M2a + polysubstance use + risk behavior (full sample). M3-sub = M3 estimated on national-module subsample. M4 = M3-sub + household adversity. M5 = M4 + community safety adversity. All models adjust for sex, grade, and race/ethnicity."
write_with_notes(s5_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS13.csv"), s13_att_notes_txt)

# Table S14 (NMPOU comparison)
write_with_notes(comp_final, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS14.csv"), s13_notes)

# Table S15 (sensitivity)
write_with_notes(s6_df, file.path(csv_dir, "TableS15.csv"), s7_sens_notes)
```
